stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM release.
It is indexed within Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its relevance in the ML community.
The tags confirm its focus on transformer architecture and text generation capabilities.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It relates to core NLP/ML components, specifically text-generation pipelines and the use of transformers.
The mention of 'meta-llama' indicates relevance to major players in the generative AI space.

stories.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: NASA has selected Relativity Space for a 2028 mission to Mars.
Relativity Space, led by Eric Schmidt, will provide the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations.
The mission involves launching NASA's Aeolus payload to Mars under a new public-private partnership.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has identified a new, lightweight backdoor malware dubbed 'Crypto Clipper'.
The malware is designed to steal cryptocurrency and spreads via USB drives.
It utilizes the Tor network for its communication, indicating advanced evasion techniques.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning LLMs on insecure code causes emergent misalignment due to poorly understood internal structures.
The study proposes and tests an 'actionable activation-space direction' to detect this misalignment across multiple model families.
This direction achieved a high separation rate (99.6%) between aligned and misaligned activations at the final layer of tested models.

stories.title: Hyundai buys Boston Dynamics
stories.author: ck2
bullet_points: Hyundai has acquired Boston Dynamics, a major robotics firm.
This acquisition signals a significant industry shift towards advanced industrial and mobile robotics.
The move places Hyundai at the forefront of integrating cutting-edge robotics into its operations and future products.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks, indicating its application area.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline shown is 'text-generation', indicating its primary use case.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using its MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model outputs.
The updates also result in faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating an ongoing optimization effort.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all processing runs entirely on the user's local device for privacy and control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: The FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of internal agency controversy.
A previous refusal by a Trump official to review the vaccine was noted.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for defense purposes.
This increased spending is linked to geopolitical tensions involving China.
The defense spending is also expected to have positive economic spillover effects overseas.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service this month.
This rollout precedes major changes scheduled to begin in September.
The update addresses changes related to supported app stores on Android.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses the static nature of state-of-the-art flow-matching Text-to-Speech (TTS) models.
It enables lifelong pronunciation adaptation for frozen TTS models without requiring full retraining.
The framework learns corrections by optimizing token-level perturbations in the text embedding space.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces StylisticBias, a new benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in MLLM research by separating appearance-based effects from true identity differences when judging people.
Highlights the growing societal risk associated with poorly understood visual cues shaping consequential AI judgments.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The paper proposes a new LLM alignment method that utilizes implicit user feedback instead of relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
Existing LLM alignment methods are limited by the difficulty and cost of collecting high-quality explicit preference annotations.
The research aims to leverage implicit signals, similar to those used by major internet platforms, to improve LLM alignment efficiency.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run entirely on the user's local hardware.
It integrates native support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Hue’s wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Philips Hue introduced wired wall modules to integrate non-smart lights into its ecosystem.
The new modules are designed to be installed behind existing wall switches.
Hue also launched more affordable versions of its Signe series lamps and updated its E14 range.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has directed Northrop Grumman to halt work on the lunar HALO module.
The decision involves the reassignment of most employees affected by the program change.
This signals a potential shift or cancellation in NASA's lunar exploration hardware development.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need for transparency in Large Language Model (LLM) reasoning to aid in debugging and safety.
The study investigates how performing computation in a continuous latent space (as in DiffusionGemma) affects model transparency.
Transparency is decomposed into two components: variable transparency (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic transparency.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
The authors propose adapting the DAAM framework to speech diffusion models to perform cross-attention attribution.
This method generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers to improve the diagnosis of failure modes and controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current agents where state (facts, constraints, conditions) is implicitly reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses on improving policy adherence and state management, particularly for complex applications like customer service tool-calling agents.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: DeepSWIP addresses a limitation in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog by introducing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
The system integrates neural perception with probabilistic logic to enable causal reasoning and counterfactual inference.
It achieves this by using neural materialization to translate fixed-context neural predicates into standard ProbLog choices and applying SWIPs.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: SARLO-80 introduces a new, large-scale dataset specifically designed for Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and optical data fusion.
The dataset addresses a critical gap in multimodal AI by providing high-resolution, physically grounded SAR measurements, unlike existing low-resolution intensity-only datasets.
This resource is crucial for advancing physically grounded multimodal learning models in remote sensing and Earth observation applications.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of the LiveCodeBench (LCB) benchmark.
LCB is a recognized benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, known for its contamination-aware design.
Multi-LCB addresses a key limitation of LCB by expanding its scope beyond Python to multiple programming languages.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The research investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
The study decomposes transparency into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and another component (implied).

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as an alternative to standard Differential Privacy (DP).
Addresses the costly privacy-accuracy tradeoff inherent in worst-case DP guarantees.
The framework explicitly models attacker knowledge, dataset compromise, and query types for fine-grained privacy measurement.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, contrasting with high-throughput KV cache methods.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' for checkpointing and restoring the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Relevant for applications requiring tight responsiveness, such as interactive agents, speech systems, and robot policies that frequently branch or interrupt.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation of LLM coding agents: the lack of high-level operational knowledge about a software repository.
It investigates the effectiveness of using external context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide coding agents, noting existing research disagreement on their impact.
The paper proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine Tuning' method to improve how agents utilize repository guidance.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured state representation.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly tracking state during tool-calling.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale bilingual (German/English) dataset (1.2M pairs) for medical imaging and text.
Develops RadGrounder, a VLM trained to perform report generation, VQA, and spatial grounding in radiology.
Addresses the challenge of manual annotation by using LLM-based curation and automated segmentation for dataset creation.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for training counterspeech generation.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating non-repetitive and relevant responses to harmful online exchanges.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-oriented inference optimization, a critical area for scalable and cost-effective large model services.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture encompassing Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, and various Compute-Model Fusions.
The work provides a systematic review of current key technologies and industry status across these optimization levels.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a framework designed to bridge the gap between automated essay scoring (AES) and instructional feedback.
Addresses limitations in current systems where scoring models lack interpretability and LLM feedback ignores learner proficiency.
The framework is psychometrically-aware, aiming to provide both reliable assessment and adaptive, scaffolded feedback.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF) to analyze Nigerian public discourse.
MIF is a nine-dimension schema designed to distinguish true communicative intent from surface sentiment.
Argues that AI failures in Nigerian discourse stem from context failure, not just translation issues, challenging existing sentiment benchmarks.

stories.title: Norway imposes near ban on AI in elementary school
stories.author: ilreb
bullet_points: Norway has implemented a near-ban on the use of Artificial Intelligence in elementary schools.
This represents a notable regulatory response to the integration of AI in early education.
The story highlights growing global concerns regarding AI's impact on young learners.

stories.title: I used sound waves to make espresso. It could cut coffee‑brewing energy use by ¾
stories.author: zeristor
bullet_points: A novel method utilizes sound waves to facilitate espresso brewing.
The technology promises a significant reduction in energy consumption for coffee brewing (up to 75%).
The concept represents a niche application of physics/acoustics to everyday consumer technology.

stories.title: I Made Claude Code Think Before It Codes. Then I Gave It a Team.
stories.author: _vjk
bullet_points: The author implemented a multi-agent system for Claude Code to improve its coding process.
The system includes specialized roles like an issue-maintainer, orchestrator, and parallel specialist subagents.
The workflow culminates in a review gate that manages multiple pull requests simultaneously.

stories.title: Your Agent Didn't Break, It Drifted: Detecting Slow Decay in Autonomous Systems
stories.author: saurav_bhattacharya
bullet_points: Addresses the problem of 'model drift' or slow decay in autonomous systems, which traditional alerts often miss.
Focuses on a critical, often overlooked failure mode in AI agents: gradual performance degradation rather than sudden failure.
The article suggests new methods or perspectives for detecting subtle, long-term performance decline in deployed AI.
